name: Billy Hill
abstract: William Joseph Hill (July 14, 1899 – December 24, 1940) was an American songwriter, violinist, and pianist who found fame writing Western songs such as "They Cut Down the Old Pine Tree", "The Last Round-Up", "Wagon Wheels", and "Empty Saddles". Hill's most popular song was "The Glory of Love", recorded by Benny Goodman in 1936 and by Peg…

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Bourgeois
about_quotes.evidence: And Billy Hill, the old songwriter that did, the one who wrote the Last Roundup and Old Spinning Wheel and those things, he was quite a fan of ours.
